user_query:
I want a small, reproducible “Resilience Memoir Insights” solution and architecture I can run locally to study how memoirists describe overcoming obstacles. Please design the solution and produce working artifacts in this workspace.

Scope and inputs
- Use the provided input/memoirs.csv to select one public-domain memoir and its author. Prefer “The Story of My Life” by Helen Keller from the official Project Gutenberg site; if that title cannot be retrieved, fall back to “Up From Slavery” by Booker T. Washington from the same official source. Do not use any third-party mirrors.
- Use the provided input/keywords.txt as the list of resilience-related keywords to aggregate (case-insensitive).

Requirements (deliverables and constraints)
1) Architecture and reproducibility doc
   - Create docs/architecture.md describing the solution architecture with these sections: Overview, Components, Data Flow, Validation & Error Handling, Reproducibility (include the exact commands you ran). Keep it concise but concrete.

2) Web retrieval and extraction (Internet required; no direct URLs in code or docs)
   - From the official Project Gutenberg landing page for the chosen title (identified by title and author), download the HTML landing page and save it as workspace/raw/page.html.
   - Parse that page’s HTML to locate the “Plain Text UTF-8” download link (or equivalent official plain-text link on that landing page), then download the full plain text of the memoir and save it as workspace/raw/memoir.txt.
   - The Internet step must traverse the landing page HTML to discover the text link (i.e., do not hardcode a direct text URL). Include light retry logic and emit meaningful messages on failure.

3) Analysis outputs (basic statistics and aggregates)
   - Compute and save workspace/summary.json with the following fields: { "title": string, "author": string, "source": "Project Gutenberg", "total_words": integer, "unique_words": integer, "avg_sentence_length_words": number, "keyword_counts": object<string,int>, "validation": { "title_author_match": boolean } }.
     • title and author must come from input/memoirs.csv for the selected row; validation.title_author_match is true only if both the title and author from the CSV appear within the first 100 non-empty lines of workspace/raw/memoir.txt (case-insensitive check).
   - Generate workspace/token_counts.csv containing the top 50 tokens by frequency (descending), with headers: token,count. Use case-insensitive tokenization, remove obvious punctuation, and exclude common English stopwords.

4) Scripts/commands and error analysis
   - Create a runnable orchestrator (e.g., scripts/run.sh or a simple CLI script) that performs the download, extraction, and analysis end-to-end and produces the files above. Document the exact invocation under Reproducibility in docs/architecture.md.
   - Demonstrate failure handling: attempt a fetch for a clearly invalid memoir (e.g., a nonsense title/author not present in input/memoirs.csv) against the official Project Gutenberg site, capture the resulting stdout/stderr or error messages to workspace/error_test.log, and summarize what failed and how your pipeline surfaces the error in the Error Handling section of docs/architecture.md.

Paths to produce (all required)
- docs/architecture.md
- workspace/raw/page.html
- workspace/raw/memoir.txt
- workspace/summary.json
- workspace/token_counts.csv
- workspace/error_test.log
- scripts/run.sh (or an equivalent runnable script/command; document its exact usage)

Notes and guardrails
- Do not include any direct URLs in the code or docs; identify the resource by title, author, and the official Project Gutenberg domain. The landing page must be fetched and parsed to discover the plain-text link.
- Keep implementation choices minimal but robust enough to pass the validation described above. The Internet step, basic aggregates, running a script, and interpreting command output/errors are all essential parts of this task. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
